lessons.title: Introduction to Cyber Security
lessons.content:
What is Cyber Security?

Cyber security is the practice of protecting systems, networks, and programs from digital attacks.

These cyberattacks are usually aimed at accessing, changing, or destroying sensitive information; extorting money from users; or interrupting normal business processes.

lessons.title: Common Cyber Threats
lessons.content:
Types of Cyber Threats

  • Phishing: The practice of sending fraudulent emails that resemble emails from reputable sources.
  • Social Engineering: A tactic that adversaries use to trick you into revealing sensitive information.
  • Malware: Software designed to gain unauthorized access or to cause damage to a computer.
  • Ransomware: A type of malicious software designed to extort money by blocking access to files or the computer system until a ransom is paid.

lessons.title: Cyber Security Best Practices
lessons.content:
How to Protect Yourself

  • Use strong, unique passwords for all your accounts.
  • Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) whenever possible.
  • Keep your software and operating systems up to date.
  • Be cautious of unsolicited emails and links.

quiz.question: What does 'CIA' stand for in Cybersecurity?
Which of the following is considered a 'Strong Password'?
What is Phishing?
options: A social engineering attack to steal data
A way to speed up the internet

quiz.question: What is the purpose of a Firewall?
options: To block unauthorized access while permitting outward communication

quiz.question: What is Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)?
options: Using multiple passwords for one account
Using at least two independent ways to verify identity
